name: @prune/churn-pin
description: F9 — git-churn cache-pin planner. Uses a FORWARD-LOOKING data source (recent git-commit frequency per file) to predict a file's near-future cache invalidation: pin low-churn files into the cacheable prefix and keep high-churn ones out, so you don't pay a cache-write for content about to change. Deterministic over caller-supplied churn counts (the host runs git, not this package). Adds a forward-looking signal the current-content cache planners lack. No regex, no model call.
